post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 245
UIC Invitational III

- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, private chats, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 12 days while each "night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned (also demonstrate that you've read the rules by posting your favorite fruit).

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Vig10 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by Scipio1.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (scipio#4398).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. I may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but I can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact me via Discord if you prefer; you can ask me about this in your role PM.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderator. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mod.
    • Do not lie to the mod.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: Scipio1 and Vote: sCiPiO1 are valid votes, but vote: scipio1, Vote: Scipio, Vote: Scipio1., Vote:Scipio1, Vote: Scipio1, and Vote: Scipio1 are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mod in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderator declares the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderator may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderator does not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderator makes a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mod for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with the moderator.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Scipio1 is the moderator of Mafia Game 245.
    • HannahW, Mathletesv, ad333, mathjay, and Socoobo have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000), and ad333's green (#55d11f).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG245OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 9.
    Current version: 9.4
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the Vig10 setup. This is an open setup.

Roles
  • 7 Vanilla Townies
  • 1 Town Vigilante
  • 2 Mafia Goons

- The factional Vigilante shot and factional mafia nightkill will always go through, regardless if one or more of them dies on the same night.
- This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 12 days from then.
- Mafia have daychat.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Vanilla Townie.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Vigilante Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Town Vigilante.

Every night, you may target a player to be killed. This kill is factional but not compulsive, meaning you may choose to not shoot on a night.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Mafia Goon.

Your partner is [player] (Mafia Goon), and you may discuss with them in this private server while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.


Alive (7)
1. awesomeming327.+
2. BobDBuilder321+
3. mathjay+ bigmath+ ad333
4. Avanae
5. EulerNTM
6. falliinqsnow-
7. Socoobo

Dead (3)

2. snoozingnewt - Vanilla Townie
3. Osmium_ - Mafia Goon


Significant Events
Dawn starts 11/19
bigmath replaces mathjay
Dawn ends, D1 starts 11/21
D1 ends, N1 starts 12/3
N1 ends, game ends 12/5
Postgame info


Votecounts

VC 1.3

Confirm Counts/Activity Checks

V/LAs and PM prods
V/LAs:
Socoobo from 11/25 to 11/28

PM Prods:

To bigmath

Warnings

Mathletesv (1/3) - posting during twilight

Announcements
D1 Deadline extended by 3 hours


Modlog
 

Resources
Mafia Win: 155
Town Win: 109
A Beginner's Guide to Being Awesome at Mafia - This MafiaWiki article explains some basic scumhunting techniques, along with strategy for being scum.
AoPS Mafia Forum Rules
Mafia FAQ - This post has a glossary of commonly used terms and an overview of how the game works.
Mafia Universe Wiki: Terminology - This is a more comprehensive list of mafia terminology; it includes some terms are not as commonly used in this forum.
This forum has posts written by experienced AoPS Mafia players on their methods for playing the game.
Automatic ISO Code - This post contains Mathletesv's code that will automatically quote all posts by a given player within a given range of posts.

Game Table of Contents

WOLF RANDS ARE FOR LOW EFFORTING SCRUBS

So, I'll need all of you to be max level towny if you're town, and if you're wolf, I'd like you to out yourself and your teammates. Although that might be unlikely. In any case, we'll crush wolves with our BIG TOWN energy.

Also, another thing. If you're worried about your reads, who do you call? Euler, of course! Sheep me pls. Not Bob.

VIG RULES

Being the vig is cool. It can also be easy. Here's the 5 basic guidelines I'd like you to follow (One of these guidelines is not serious).

- Don't shoot me: I'm the towniest town to ever town.
- Sheep my PoE: Duuuuuh!
- "Towncore sucks, let me clear it out!": The towncore is towny. Even if you think there's a deepwolf don't shoot because its the towncore and we'll need them if they're not .
- Don't shoot scummy scums, shoot Lame Larrys: If scum exists in the deep, dark echelons of PoE, they don't have a chance at escaping the box. However, those few slots that are on the fringes of PoE? Aim well, and shoot those slots. There's a good chance you can catch those few "deepwolving but kinda not" wolves there.
- WIFOM your butt off: The great part about being the extra town KP? You have choices. Maaany choices. So WIFOM them out! That'll scare the living hell out of scum. It's not only be fun but it'll make scum flub and lose their camouflage.

If you follow these, this'll be what you look like post-game:



Everyone loves Arnold Schwarzenegger.

post_rendered:
Oh, and something I wanted to say. Euler touched on this a bit in his opening wallpost but it was kinda buried in the middle. DO NOT SHOOT SOMEONE THAT IS LIKELY TO BE HANGED THE NEXT DAY. If they are at the bottom end of the PoE, we've likely spent discussion time on them to justify it and reach a consensus. If you shoot that person, that means we will have wasted all that previous discussion time, no longer have a consensus hang, and we'll have to spend more time haggling over who to hang next instead of having that time to consider other things. So the conclusion is that you should ALWAYS SHOOT IN THE MIDDLE OF THE POE. Too far to the top means you'll likely hit town, too far to the bottom means it'll be quite inefficient in terms of discussion time.

Thanks for listening to my TED talk.

Oh, and something I wanted to say. Euler touched on this a bit in his opening wallpost but it was kinda buried in the middle. DO NOT SHOOT SOMEONE THAT IS LIKELY TO BE HANGED THE NEXT DAY. If they are at the bottom end of the PoE, we've likely spent discussion time on them to justify it and reach a consensus. If you shoot that person, that means we will have wasted all that previous discussion time, no longer have a consensus hang, and we'll have to spend more time haggling over who to hang next instead of having that time to consider other things. So the conclusion is that you should ALWAYS SHOOT IN THE MIDDLE OF THE POE. Too far to the top means you'll likely hit town, too far to the bottom means it'll be quite inefficient in terms of discussion time.

Socoobo wrote:
Oh, and something I wanted to say. Euler touched on this a bit in his opening wallpost but it was kinda buried in the middle. DO NOT SHOOT SOMEONE THAT IS LIKELY TO BE HANGED THE NEXT DAY. If they are at the bottom end of the PoE, we've likely spent discussion time on them to justify it and reach a consensus. If you shoot that person, that means we will have wasted all that previous discussion time, no longer have a consensus hang, and we'll have to spend more time haggling over who to hang next instead of having that time to consider other things. So the conclusion is that you should ALWAYS SHOOT IN THE MIDDLE OF THE POE. Too far to the top means you'll likely hit town, too far to the bottom means it'll be quite inefficient in terms of discussion time.

Thanks for listening to my TED talk.

Doing a reread and I actually strongly disagree with this
if we've spent time talking over why someone should be elimmed or why someone is a wolf then they're a good shot. if you don't shoot them then we take the next day and either quickhammer them which would be bad, or we spend over a week talking about them again. if we've talked about them and then don't kill them that day, they're a safe target.

shooting someone else and leaving us with the most suspicious person who is still alive and we are /still/ talking about? not productive.

Guys stop. We don't hang inactives here. Remember arood from 224:
aroodman wrote:
Okay, this game wasn't exactly a big offender in this regard, but it sure made the problem clear. Been wanting to scream about this recently.

You know all those times you resign to the fate of "PoE hangs" by saying "This guy will never get out of PoE so we should just kill him"? Makes sense, right? Your win condition is to narrow PoE, right?

No, your win condition is to kill scum. Kill. Scum.

First of all, while there is such a thing as a truly unresolvable slot, chances are only one or two of the slots you've ever called unresolvable have actually been unresolvable. The other cases? You're just lazy. Or tunneled. Or both. This game saw just that, with early acceptance of the deaths of two townies, one who barely registered as scummy for oddly placed lolcatting a couple of times at worst, and one who was killed for being off-meta. These would be acceptable arguments if they were the results of examining the entire playerlist and finding the others townier, but they most definitely weren't.

And guess what? If you were looking for reasons not to hang people as well as reasons to hang people, you could've looked closer and seen that nike was obvtown. He had genuinely no idea what was going on, he knew he was probably screwed, and he was still trying his absolute hardest to somehow help town in any way possible, even when he couldn’t tell what that would even mean. The extremes of purity and WIM under the most extreme pressure. He broke at a time where it was more than reasonable to break.

Second of all, even if you think you have a winning PoE, you should still aim for accuracy. You will make wrong PoEs. That is a fact of life. That is inevitable. You shouldn't just stop all work the moment you think you've made it.

If you catch and kill scum, you can remove more townies from PoE by interactions. If you truly care to play the game even after you think you've won by PoE, you'll probably clear even more people just in discussion. Think of all the clears you could access just by deciding to play.

If you have a wrong PoE of size 4 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F5, and you'll have two chances to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 5 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F3, and you'll have one chance to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 6 in a 13-player game, too late GG you're dead. The narrower you get PoE, the sooner you'll realize it's wrong, if it is, and the more time you'll have to correct it.

All of this is to say that "but we have to" hangs are bad even when you think you have the game locked down. Except, in the case of cw, you didn't already have a winning PoE. You said "Hey, in the worst case, this can't make our jobs any harder, right? We'd never be able to clear this guy anyway." No, it made your jobs harder. You could've flipped a wolf that would clear cw (and hptl would’ve cleared cw). You could've looked at more than just stylistic elements of cw's play. You could've looked through a hundred ways of reading cw. You cannot decide on D1 that anyone actually actively competently engaging the game will be permanently unresolvable. Under no circumstances.
